title: Cross - Tort Law and the American Economy
description: Frank B. Cross — Tort Law and the American Economy (Minnesota Law Review, 2011)
show_notes:
Frank B. Cross — Tort Law and the American Economy (Minnesota Law Review, 2011)

Cross tests the widely asserted claim that tort law undermines American economic growth by examining state-level economic data against tort-law indices created by two pro-defendant organizations. Despite the common narrative that pro-plaintiff tort regimes discourage business investment and innovation, he finds no evidence that states with more defendant-friendly tort law perform better economically, suggesting that the economic case for tort reform has been significantly overstated.

Chapters:
2:40 I. The Controversy Over Tort Law
26:17 II. Research on Tort Law and the Economy
37:36 III. The Measures of Tort Law Used in This Study
51:18 IV. An Analysis of Economic Effects of State Tort Law
1:15:18 V. Pathways for Tort Law to Affect the General Economy
